title: MCP vs API: The protocol every developer needs to know
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a new protocol designed for connecting LLMs and AI agents to tools, data, and systems without manual hardcoding. Unlike traditional APIs, MCP enables models to autonomously discover and use tools while supporting middleware interactions with multiple services like Gmail, Notion, and Jira.
writeup: This video contrasts the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with traditional APIs as a way to connect LLMs and AI agents to tools, data, and systems. MCP removes the need to manually hardcode integrations and lets models autonomously discover and use tools, functioning as middleware across multiple services such as Gmail, Notion, and Jira. The core takeaway: where APIs require developers to wire up each connection by hand, MCP standardizes tool calling so agents can find and invoke capabilities on their own.
